title: 10 best herbal creams for ringworm

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, specifically its ability to target Candida and Malassezia species. The cream's active ingredient, melaleuca alternifolia oil, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal cells, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. This natural remedy is also gentle on skin, making it an effective alternative to harsh chemical treatments. By applying tea tree creams topically to affected areas, ringworm symptoms such as redness, itching, and crusting can be quickly alleviated.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Tea tree oil's potent antifungal properties help combat the growth and spread of fungal organisms that cause ringworm, such as Trichophyton, Epidermophyton, and Microsporum.
Antibacterial activity: Additionally, tea tree oil's antibacterial properties can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that may arise from ringworm lesions or compromised skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a more comfortable and faster healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea tree plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have antifungal properties that can help combat fungal infections like ringworm.

Barks: Tea tree bark contains a high concentration of terpenes, including cineole, which has been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, making it effective against ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves measuring approximately one cup.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or oven at low temperature for six hours.
Steep two tablespoons of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in one cup of boiling distilled water for ten minutes.
Strain and cool the infused liquid, then mix it with four tablespoons of melted coconut oil and two teaspoons of beeswax.
Heat the mixture at low temperature until the beeswax is fully incorporated, taking approximately fifteen to twenty minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which enable it to combat the fungal infection that causes ringworm. The aloe vera gel contains compounds such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to exhibit potent antifungal activity against various fungal species, including those that cause ringworm. Additionally, aloe creams help to soothe and calm the affected skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections, providing relief to affected skin.

Antifungal property: The antifungal agents present in aloe vera, such as aloin and acemannan, combat fungal growth and inhibit the spread of ringworm, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of recurrence.

Wound-healing property: Aloe vera's rich concentration of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids accelerates wound closure by stimulating collagen production, improving skin elasticity, and enhancing tissue repair, which is particularly beneficial in treating ringworm lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots are valued for their ability to inhibit fungal growth and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating ringworm infections.
Barks: The barks of the aloe plant contain bioactive compounds that exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties, helping to combat ringworm-causing fungi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and extract the gel by cutting off the thick outer rind and squeezing gently releasing the clear inner gel.
Combine 20 grams of aloe vera gel with 10 grams of calendula flowers and 5 grams of tea tree essential oil in a heatproof bowl.
Add 50 milliliters of warm coconut oil to the mixture and stir until well combined and heated through for 30 minutes.
Pour the infused mixture into sterilized glass jars and refrigerate for at least 24 hours allowing the cream to thicken.
After the thickening process, apply a thin layer of the herbal aloe vera cream directly to ringworm affected areas two times daily for 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Neem creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. Neem contains azadirachtin, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause ringworm. Additionally, neem's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections. The cream also promotes a healthy skin environment, allowing the body's natural healing processes to take over, ultimately clearing up the infection and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Neem's antifungal compounds, such as nimbin and azadirachtin, inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, effectively treating ringworm by targeting the underlying cause.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Neem's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery and relieving discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Neem's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate skin damage caused by ringworm, helping to prevent scarring and promote healthy tissue regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds, which help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Barks: Contain salannin, a compound with antifungal properties that can aid in treating ringworm skin lesions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh azadirachta indica leaves from healthy trees for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the leaves in shade or using a low-temperature oven for at least 24 hours to preserve their properties.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for maximum extraction.
Mix one part of the azadirachta indica powder with three parts of coconut oil and apply heat gently while stirring until it melts completely.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any impurities before filling and storing in sterilized containers at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, specifically targeting Candida albicans, the most common cause of ringworm. The cream's active ingredient, calendula, inhibits fungal growth and promotes skin healing. Additionally, pot marigold's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with ringworm infections. As a result, topical application of herbal pot marigold creams can effectively alleviate symptoms and promote the body's natural healing process to combat ringworm infections.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Calendula, the key compound found in pot marigold cream, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms such as Candida albicans, which can cause ringworm, by disrupting their cell membranes and preventing them from producing toxins.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in pot marigold cream have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery and minimizing the risk of infection spreading.

Wound-healing properties: Calendula has been traditionally used to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue oxygenation, and enhancing fibroblast proliferation, which helps accelerate the process of resolving ringworm lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties in the roots help to combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes' ability to inhibit fungal growth and promote healing makes them a valuable addition to medicinal creams for ringworm treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at dawn on a sunny day for maximum potency in 2 hours.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a single layer at room temperature in 3 days.
Infuse the dried calendula flowers in cold-pressed olive oil for 2 weeks in darkness.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids in 1 hour.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler at low heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with ringworm because of its antimicrobial properties, which allow it to combat fungal infections that cause this condition. The cream's active ingredients, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, work together to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, herbal licorice creams often contain soothing agents like aloe vera and tea tree oil, which help to calm itching and discomfort associated with ringworm.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal licorice creams contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.

Antifungal properties: Licorice root extract has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungal species, including those that cause ringworm, making it a potential treatment for the infection.

Sothing and protective properties: Herbal licorice creams often contain soothing ingredients like glycerin and panthenol, which can help calm and protect the affected skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been traditionally used in traditional medicine to treat fungal infections such as ringworm, thanks to its antimicrobial and antifungal compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder from a reputable source.
Mix the powdered root with 50 milliliters of distilled water and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl.
Add 20 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax to the liquid mixture and heat gently until melted.
Pour the warm mixture into sterilized jars and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Olive creams helps with ringworm because of their unique combination of antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in herbal olive creams, such as vitamin E and polyphenols, work together to reduce inflammation and promote healing of affected skin. Additionally, the cream's antifungal properties help to combat the fungus that causes ringworm, while also soothing any itching or discomfort associated with the condition. This makes olive creams a natural and effective solution for treating ringworm and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal olive creams contain antimicrobial agents that help to combat fungal infections like ringworm, preventing the spread of the infection and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: These creams possess anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, making it more comfortable for individuals affected by the condition.

Antifungal properties: The antifungal agents present in herbal olive creams help to destroy or inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens responsible for causing ringworm, effectively treating the infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, olive roots have been traditionally used to treat fungal infections like ringworm due to their antimicrobial properties.
Barks: The barks of the olive tree contain tannins and flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungi, including those that cause ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 100g of fresh olive leaves olea europaea for two weeks.
Blend the dried olive leaves with 20g of coconut oil in a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Transfer the mixture to a double boiler and heat it gently at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Mix the resulting infusion with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter, then pour into sterilized containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which effectively combat the fungal infection that causes this common skin condition. The active compounds in garlic, such as allicin and ajoene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including species responsible for ringworm. By applying topical garlic cream directly to affected areas, the fungus is unable to spread and multiply, promoting healing and reducing inflammation, ultimately helping to clear up the infection.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Herbal garlic creams contain compounds like allicin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal infections, including ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Garlic's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm lesions, promoting faster healing and reduced discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of garlic creams can help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of scarring and promoting a more rapid recovery from ringworm infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the garlic plant have antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections like ringworm, making them a popular choice for natural remedies.
Rhizomes: Garlic rhizomes contain sulfur compounds and other bioactive compounds that aid in preventing fungal growth and promoting skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry allium sativum bulbs for at least seven days to ensure potency.
Grind 20 grams of dried allium sativum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 10 milliliters of beeswax until well combined.
Heat the mixture at 65 degrees celsius for one hour to facilitate infusions and emulsification.
Strain and filter the cream through cheesecloth or a coffee filter before pouring into sterilized containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties. The natural compounds present in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, possess strong antimicrobial activity that can effectively combat fungal infections like ringworm. These compounds can penetrate deep into the skin to target the infection at its source, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell growth. As a result, herbal ginger creams can help soothe and clear up ringworm lesions, promoting a faster recovery and reduced risk of reinfection.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Ginger's antifungal compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, including Trichophyton, Epidermophyton, and Microsporum species.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm, making it more comfortable to treat and manage.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidants help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals generated during fungal infection, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which exhibit antifungal properties, making them effective against ringworm.
Barks: Containing resins and volatile oils, the barks help to soothe and calm skin irritations caused by ringworm, promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of fresh ginger rhizome using 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a slow cooker for four hours.
Strain the extracted mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to the strained liquid and melt it gently over low heat with continuous stirring until fully incorporated.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of 65 degrees celsius before whipping it into a smooth cream consistency using an electric mixer for five minutes.
Fill sterile glass jars with the final herbal zingiber officinale cream, leaving one inch headspace, and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months or freeze for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which are derived from the plant's active compounds such as triterpenoid saponins. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth and spread of fungal infections like ringworm, while also promoting healthy skin cell regeneration. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm, allowing for a more comfortable healing process. remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Kansas coneflower contains antifungal compounds such as triterpenoids and saponins, which inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, helping to reduce symptoms like itching and inflammation.

remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root powder and mix it with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the echinacea to infuse into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of shea butter to the infused oil and stir until melted and smooth, then cool slightly.
Whip the cooled mixture with an electric mixer or whisk for 2-3 minutes until it thickens into a creamy consistency suitable for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with ringworm because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause ringworm, while also reducing inflammation and itching associated with the infection. Additionally, turmeric's natural antioxidant properties help to promote healthy skin and tissue repair, making it an effective natural remedy for treating ringworm and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: A polyphenol compound found in turmeric, curcumin has potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat ringworm by reducing fungal growth and inflammation associated with the infection.

Vitamin C: Turmeric creams often contain vitamin C, which plays a crucial role in boosting the immune system and enhancing the body's natural defense against infections like ringworm, thereby facilitating faster recovery.

Antioxidants: Turmeric contains various antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by ringworm, promoting a healthy environment for the skin to recover.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and combating fungal infections associated with ringworm.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain curcuminoids, which exhibit antifungal activity, helping to combat the fungus that causes ringworm and promoting wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Collect 20g of dried turmeric roots and grind into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 15g of the turmeric powder with 50g of coconut oil and stir well for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes, allowing the oil to infuse with the turmeric's active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 10 minutes.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which allow it to effectively combat the fungal infection that causes this skin condition. The cream's active ingredients, such as beta-carotene and fatty acids, work together to reduce inflammation and promote healing, ultimately clearing up the infection and promoting healthy skin. Additionally, saw palmetto has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe itching and discomfort associated with ringworm.

Antioxidant: Saw palmetto contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature aging and skin disorders.

Barks: Containing compounds with antiseptic and astringent properties, helping to soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including organic serenoa repens leaves dried at 50°c for 2 hours and virgin coconut oil in a 3:1 ratio.
Crush the dried serenoa repens leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 15 minutes.
Combine the crushed leaf powder with the virgin coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture every 10 minutes as it infuses for an additional 20 minutes to ensure optimal extraction of herbal properties.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to sterilized glass jars for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to effectively combat fungal infections like ringworm. When applied topically, these oils create an environment that is inhospitable to the fungus, slowing down its growth and eventually causing it to die off. As a result, herbal thyme creams can help alleviate symptoms of ringworm, including itching, redness, and inflammation, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Thyme contains antimicrobial compounds such as thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause ringworm, by disrupting their cell membranes and preventing them from producing essential nutrients.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections, making it more comfortable to manage the condition.

Barks: Containing high amounts of tannins, thyme barks help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them an effective addition to medicinal creams for ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and dry them in a warm dark place for at least 24 hours.
Grind dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle and set aside.
Mix 20 grams of ground thymus vulgaris powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water and stir until well combined.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to create a gentle steam distillation process and extract essential oils.
Allow the cooled mixture to thicken for at least 2 hours before transferring it to glass jars and storing in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with ringworm because they contain powerful antifungal compounds that effectively combat the fungal infections causing this common skin condition. The cream's active ingredients, such as eucalyptol and cajeput oil, work to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and prevent the spread of the infection. Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with ringworm, providing relief and comfort for the affected area.

remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and equipment by submerging them in boiling water for at least 15 minutes.
Combine 250ml of coconut oil and 100g of beeswax in a double boiler and heat until melted and smooth.
Add 20 drops of eucalyptus globulus essential oil to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Remove from heat and let cool to around 65°c before pouring into sterilized jars and sealing tightly.
Allow the creams to set for at least 30 minutes at room temperature before storing in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with ringworm because of its natural antifungal properties, which work to combat the fungal infection that causes this common skin condition. The cream's active ingredients, such as citral and geraniol, penetrate deep into the skin, breaking down the fungal cell walls and inhibiting their growth. This ultimately leads to a reduction in ringworm symptoms, including redness, itching, and rashes.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass oil can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster healing process.

remedy_recipe: Gather 50 grams of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and 20 grams of coconut oil.
Combine the dried leaves in a heat-resistant glass container with 100 milliliters of olive oil.
Steam-distill the mixture at 100 degrees celsius for two hours to obtain essential oil.
Mix one teaspoon of the obtained essential oil with 50 grams of beeswax and five tablespoons of coconut oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler and stir until it reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius before pouring into containers for later use.

Infuse the dried tea leaves in hot water to create a strong tea decoction.
Strain the tea decoction and mix it with coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat until the beeswax melts and the ingredients combine smoothly.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken before transferring it to airtight containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sandalwood creams helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which target the fungus that causes this skin infection. The cream's active compounds, such as beta-santalol and alpha-santalol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal cells, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin. Additionally, sandalwood's antimicrobial properties help prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate ringworm symptoms. remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried santalum album powder from a reputable supplier.
Combine the powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 10 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer for 5 minutes to incorporate air.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and seal tightly, allowing it to mature for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with ringworm because of its potent anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial properties. The boswellic acids in frankincense cream reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm, allowing for faster healing and relief. remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried boswellia serrata powder into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground boswellia powder with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 2 drops of tea tree essential oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax pellets to the mixture, stirring until the beeswax melts for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool at room temperature for 20 minutes before whipping with a hand mixer for 30 seconds.
Transfer the cream to an airtight container and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks or freeze for longer storage.

The cream's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, work by disrupting the growth and spread of fungal cells, reducing symptoms like itching, redness, and scaling. Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon creams' anti-inflammatory properties soothe and reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm, promoting faster healing and alleviating discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground cinnamomum verum powder with 50 grams of coconut oil and 25 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 100 degrees fahrenheit before adding 10 grams of aloe vera gel and 5 grams of vitamin e oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in an airtight container for at least 24 hours to allow for proper curing before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with ringworm because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, including salicylic acid and tannins, work together to soothe and calm affected skin, reducing redness and itching associated with the fungal infection. Additionally, witch hazel's natural antifungal compounds help combat the growth of ringworm fungus, promoting healthy healing and preventing further spread of the condition. This makes American witch hazel creams a popular and effective treatment option for those suffering from ringworm.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to combat the fungal infection causing ringworm, inhibiting its growth and promoting wound healing.

Phenolic Compounds: Phenolic compounds in Witch Hazel exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections, allowing for a more comfortable treatment experience.

Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Witch Hazel have antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the infection from spreading and promote tissue repair, facilitating faster recovery from ringworm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have antimicrobial properties that help to combat fungal infections like ringworm, allowing them to be effective in reducing symptoms and promoting healing.
Buds: The buds contain tannins, which have antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects that can help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry hamamelis virginiana leaves, collecting 2 cups of dried material for use.
Combine the dried hamamelis virginiana leaves with 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan, heating mixture over low heat.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes, allowing the hamamelis virginiana to infuse into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, retaining the infused oil and discarding solids after 1 hour.
Mix the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter, heating the blend in a double boiler for 10 minutes to create a creamy consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for ringworm are topical treatments made from natural ingredients such as tea tree oil, neem oil, and aloe vera that provide an alternative to conventional antifungal creams. These herbal creams help to alleviate symptoms of ringworm by reducing inflammation, soothing itching, and promoting healthy skin. For example, tea tree oil's antifungal properties can help to kill the fungus causing the infection, while aloe vera soothes and calms the affected area. By using these herbal creams, individuals with ringworm can experience relief from discomfort and embarrassment, improving their overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ringworm with herbal creams is by applying a cream containing tea tree oil, which has antifungal properties that target the root cause of the infection. Additionally, creams infused with burdock root, calendula, and aloe vera can soothe the affected area, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Apply the cream twice daily, making sure to cover the entire infected area.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ringworm that are treated with herbal creams are fungal infections such as athlete's foot, jock itch, and pityriasis versicolor. These conditions are caused by different species of fungi but share similar symptoms like redness, itching, and scaly rashes. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and lavender oil have been found to be effective in treating these fungal infections, providing relief from itching and inflammation while promoting skin healing.
